FAQsJournalContact

Servant Leadership Devotional

This devotional is built on a simple, demanding idea:

Lead others the way you would want to be led.

Often called the Golden Rule, this principle sits at the foundation of Servant Leadership. These daily reflections connect Scripture to the real pressures of leadership—power, responsibility, trust, restraint, and care for people.

The goal is not inspiration for inspiration's sake, but formation. Each entry invites leaders to slow down, examine their assumptions, and renew how they think about influence and responsibility.

👉 To understand the leadership framework behind this devotional, read The Golden Rule Has Always Been a Leadership Strategy.

Today's Devotional

April 18, 2026

April 18, 2026

Willing Surrender

Matthew 26:36–39 (ESV)

Unable to load scripture text: Unexpected token '<', "<!doctype "... is not valid JSON

Read Matthew 26:36–39 (ESV) on ESV.org

Reflection

Jesus enters the moment where cost becomes immediate.

There is no distance between what is coming and what is felt. He does not deny the weight of it.

Servant leadership does not ignore difficulty. It faces it directly.

Jesus expresses the tension clearly. There is desire for another way, yet there is full alignment with the will of the Father.

This is not passive acceptance. It is deliberate surrender.

Leadership often reaches moments where the right path is also the hardest one.

Jesus shows that clarity does not remove struggle. It anchors obedience within it.

Practical Application

  • Acknowledge a decision that carries weight.
  • Choose alignment over avoidance.
  • Act with clarity even when it is difficult.

Takeaways

  • Surrender aligns leadership with purpose.
  • Clarity does not remove difficulty.

Closing Thought

Strong leadership moves forward even when the cost is clear.

Scripture quotations are from the ESV® Bible (The Holy Bible, English Standard Version®), © 2001 by Crossway, a publishing ministry of Good News Publishers. ESV Text Edition: 2026. The ESV text may not be quoted in any publication made available to the public by a Creative Commons license. The ESV may not be translated in whole or in part into any other language. Used by permission. All rights reserved.
